text.title: Legal Compliance Checklist — Stage-by-Stage Items
text.summary: Compliance checklist organized by business stage: Day 1, Month 1, Year 1, and Ongoing, plus website-specific requirements.

rule.statement: Complete all Day 1 items before accepting any payment or collecting any user data.
rule.rationale: Legal and data-protection obligations attach at the moment of business activity, not at scale. Pre-revenue compliance is cheaper than retrofitted compliance.

list.item: Business entity chosen and registered (LLC, S-Corp, C-Corp, or sole proprietor)
EIN obtained or applied for
Business bank account opened (separate from personal)
Terms of Service drafted and published
Privacy Policy drafted (GDPR/CCPA-aware) and published
Cookie Policy created if website uses cookies
Refund/Return Policy documented
Business license obtained if required by state
Terms of Service and Privacy Policy linked in website footer
Contact information publicly accessible on site
Consent mechanism implemented for personal data collection
Marketing email opt-out mechanism working and tested
GDPR compliance verified if serving EU customers (consent, DPO contact if required)
CCPA compliance verified if serving California customers (Do Not Sell link if applicable)
PCI-DSS compliance verified for payment processing
Trademark search completed for business name and logo
Licenses for any third-party content (images, fonts, code) confirmed
Tax professional (CPA/accountant) engaged
Quarterly tax estimate process established
Sales tax nexus determined by state
Contractor agreements finalized and on file
CAN-SPAM compliance verified for email marketing (physical address, unsubscribe)
Industry-specific regulations identified and documented
General liability insurance obtained
Cyber liability insurance obtained if handling user data
Trademark registration initiated for high-value brand assets

rule.statement: Establish a quarterly policy-review cycle. Assign a calendar reminder and treat it as a recurring sprint task, not an optional maintenance item.
rule.rationale: Policies become inaccurate as the product evolves. A privacy policy written at launch that does not reflect current data flows is a compliance liability and erodes user trust.

list.item: Regulatory change monitoring (FTC, EDPB, ICO, state privacy laws, EU AI Act)
Policies updated within 30 days of material regulatory change
Data retention policy enforced and deletion procedure tested
GDPR right-to-deletion process (DSAR) verified quarterly
Data breach notification plan reviewed and contacts current
Compliance documentation stored securely with version history
Third-party vendor list reconciled against privacy policy disclosures
SSL/HTTPS installed on all pages
Cookie consent banner present and compliant (no pre-ticked boxes, equal-prominence reject button)
WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility verified
Data access/correction/deletion request process documented and accessible
Privacy policies for all analytics and tracking tools verified
CDN/hosting provider data practices reviewed
